title: Provide a simple way to run hooks at different stages of container setup
body:
### Feature request description

I would like to be able to run hooks at different stages of the container setup in an easy way.

A typical example is to set up firewall rules on a container. Thanks to the excellent help in https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/27099 , I was able to set i) a hook, ii) write a script, iii) load the hook, iv) annotate the `podman run` command, in order to set firewall rules on a container (see example at https://github.com/jerabaul29/2025_podman_iptable_rules , feedback welcome :) ).

I would like to be able to do this in "one shot" / a simpler way.

### Suggest potential solution

Would it be possible to provide a simpler way of running scripts at different stages of the container setup?

I think a possible solution would be to provide extra command line arguments to `podman run`, to provide the path to a script to run at a given time in container setup. For example `--path-script-createContainer SOME_PATH` to run the script at `SOME_PATH` at the `createContainer` stage. Other stages could be provided too.

### Have you considered any alternatives?

Now this can be done with hook + script + annotation, but this is quite a bit of "magics" and "layers of indirection" just for running a script at a given stage - maybe this could be possible to do with a simpler API for the end user?

### Additional context

_No response_

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
the upstream team has discussed this and we do not wish to exploit more hook capability ourselves.
Perfectly fine - and using OCI hooks works fine anyways. Closing.
